pattern: Once the section boundaries are pinned to consecutive pairs of seats, the count of arrangements collapses to a product of the plant-run widths between those pairs.
hints: A valid section always contains exactly two seats, so first check whether the total seat count is even and at least two -- if not, no division exists at all.
List the positions of the seats. Pairing them up in order (1st with 2nd, 3rd with 4th, and so on) fixes where every section starts and ends -- there is no freedom left in how seats are grouped.

The only real choice is where to drop the divider between one finished pair and the next: any of the plant positions between the last seat of a pair and the first seat of the following pair works, so multiply those counts together (mod 1e9+7) across every gap.

edge_inputs.description: no seats at all, so there is nothing to divide into two-seat sections
an odd seat count makes any valid division impossible
exactly one pair with no plants to spare -- only the no-op division is valid
minimum-length corridor with no seat at all
two pairs directly adjacent with zero plants between them still allow exactly one placement, not zero
several plants sit between two pairs, multiplying the number of valid divider placements
three chained pairs whose differing gap widths must multiply together
many tightly packed pairs, each boundary forced to a single placement

stress.description: long random corridor of seats and plants near the max length, exercising both the parity check and long products
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    chars = ['S' if rng.random() < 0.35 else 'P' for _ in range(n)]
    return [''.join(chars)]
